questions.question: What is the purpose of the `document.querySelector()` method in DOM manipulation?
questions.hint: It is used to select the first element that matches the specified CSS selector.

questions.question: What is the difference between `document.getElementById()` and `document.querySelector()`?
questions.hint: While both methods can select elements, `document.getElementById()` always returns the first element with the specified ID, whereas `document.querySelector()` can select elements based on a CSS selector.

questions.question: How can you get all the paragraph elements within a document using the `document` object?
questions.hint: You can use `document.querySelectorAll('p')` to get a NodeList of all paragraph elements.
